old_claim.note: Carbon Accounting Software provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct Carbon Accounting Software to Carbon Capture and Storage edge remains because CCS is an industrial separation, transport, injection, and storage technology rather than a software-dependent accounting workflow.

source_shape.source_locator: EPA describes Class VI wells as wells used to inject carbon dioxide into deep rock formations for long-term underground storage as part of geologic sequestration.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source frames carbon capture and storage around geologic sequestration infrastructure and underground injection wells.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the cited CCS source supports physical storage infrastructure and not GHG reporting software, it does not justify carbon-accounting software as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Carbon Capture and Storage depended on Carbon Accounting Software, conflating measurement/reporting tooling with the physical CCS technology stack.
ontology_after: Carbon Capture and Storage remains scoped to chemistry, separation, drilling, and geologic storage capabilities; carbon accounting is contextual reporting infrastructure outside the prerequisite chain.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: removed a temporal and semantic overreach edge. Preserved: the node still depends on advanced chemistry and deep drilling technology.
would_reject_if: The Carbon Capture and Storage node is narrowed to software-managed carbon-credit accounting systems rather than physical CCS systems.
A source shows CCS separation and geologic sequestration technically require carbon-accounting software.
A new monitored_carbon_storage_reporting_platforms node is introduced and intentionally depends on carbon_accounting_software.
short_reason: Carbon-accounting software is reporting context for CCS, not a prerequisite for physical capture and storage systems.
